title: Allhomes data extraction brief
text: allhomes R package extracts historical sales data Australian property website Allhomes.com.au. Starting version 0.4.0, package uses Allhomes’ Apollo GraphQL API instead scraping static HTML pages, Allhomes shifted dynamic data loading. approach queries /graphql endpoint structured JSON responses, improving data access. package relies httr2 HTTP requests tidyverse data manipulation.

title: Allhomes data extraction brief
text: Data extraction involves sending GraphQL query Allhomes’ API. Key inputs include suburb slug (specific format like “suburb-state-postcode”), year (optional), pagination parameters. API returns sales records JSON, package processes tidy data frame. Users interact via get_past_sales_data(), handles query formatting internally.

title: Allhomes data extraction brief
text: Persisted queries: package uses predefined GraphQL query identified SHA256 hash. hash ensures server recognises query can change server-side, potentially breaking requests. Variables: Queries include locality (suburb slug type), filters (e.g., minimum bedrooms/bathrooms), duration (specific year history), sorting (sale age), pagination (page number size). Request structure: GET request /graphql operation name, JSON-encoded variables, extensions (including hash). Headers specify operation proper routing.

title: Allhomes data extraction brief
text: Reliability depends SHA256 hash remaining unchanged; Allhomes updates server-side, requests fail, requiring package updates. Data fetched single request default limit 5000 records, may truncate results high-volume suburbs. Users can increase limit via get_past_sales_data(), function warns records match limit. Updates via pull requests welcome address issues.

title: Median sale prices across Sydney Leichhardt suburbs in 2021
text: vignette demonstrates use allhomes package extract analyse historical property sales data allhomes.com.au. ’ll focus exploring median property sale prices across suburbs Sydney Leichhardt area 2021. analysing sales data multiple suburbs, ’ll create visualisations understand price distributions identify patterns local property market. allhomes package provides access detailed sales data, including property features, sale prices, dates. example shows collect data multiple suburbs, clean , perform basic exploratory data analysis understand median price variations.

title: Median sale prices across Sydney Leichhardt suburbs in 2021
text: First, load required packages. ’ll use allhomes Allhomes sales data extraction, tidyverse data manipulation visualisation, ggbeeswarm creating bee swarm plots.
code: library(allhomes) library(tidyverse) library(ggbeeswarm)

title: Median sale prices across Sydney Leichhardt suburbs in 2021
text: get_past_sales_data() function retrieves sales data specific suburb year. ’ll collect data suburbs Leichhardt SA3 area using internal divisions_NSW dataset identify relevant suburbs.
code: # Get all Leichhardt suburbs suburbs <- divisions_NSW |>     filter(sa3_name_2016 == "Leichhardt") |>     unite(suburb, division, state, sep = ", ") |>     pull(suburb)  # Display the suburbs we're analysing suburbs #> [1] "Annandale, NSW"    "Balmain, NSW"      "Balmain East, NSW" #> [4] "Birchgrove, NSW"   "Leichhardt, NSW"   "Lilyfield, NSW"    #> [7] "Rozelle, NSW"  # Get data for Leichhardt suburbs data <- suburbs |> map(get_past_sales_data, year = 2021L) |> bind_rows() #> Warning in .f(.x[[i]], ...): No sales data for suburb 'Balmain East, NSW'

title: Median sale prices across Sydney Leichhardt suburbs in 2021
text: Let’s examine collected data. ’ll look total number sales, data completeness, basic statistics suburb property type. Summary sales data suburb property type (2021)
code: # Summary statistics by suburb and property type data_summary <- data %>%     group_by(division, property_type) |>     summarise(         total_sales = n(),         sales_with_price = sum(!is.na(price) & price > 0),         median_price = median(price, na.rm = TRUE),         mean_price = mean(price, na.rm = TRUE),         .groups = "drop") |>     arrange(division, property_type)  # Display summary table data_summary |>     knitr::kable(         caption = "Summary of sales data by suburb and property type (2021)",         digits = 0,         format.args = list(big.mark = ","))

title: Median sale prices across Sydney Leichhardt suburbs in 2021
text: Now ’ll analyse median sale prices across Leichhardt suburbs. ’ll filter valid data create visualisation showing price distributions median values different property types.
code: # Prepare data for plotting plot_data <- data |>     filter(!is.na(price), price > 1e3) |>  # Remove invalid prices     mutate(median_price = median(price), .by = property_type)  # Calculate median per property type  # Create bee swarm plot with median lines plot_data |>     ggplot(aes(division, price, colour = property_type)) +     geom_quasirandom(dodge.width = 0.8, alpha = 0.7) +     geom_errorbar(         aes(ymin = median_price, ymax = median_price),          position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),         linewidth = 1) +     scale_y_continuous(         labels = scales::label_dollar(scale = 1e-6, suffix = "M")) +     labs(         title = "Property sale prices across Leichhardt suburbs (2021)",         subtitle = "Bee swarm plot showing individual sales with median price lines",         x = "Leichhardt suburb",          y = "Sales price (AUD)",         colour = "Property type") +     theme_minimal()

title: Median sale prices across Sydney Leichhardt suburbs in 2021
text: visualisation data analysis, can observe: Price variation suburb: clear differences median property prices suburbs within Leichhardt area, reflecting local market conditions property characteristics. Property type differences: expected, different property types (houses, units, etc.) show distinct price distributions, houses generally resulting higher prices. Data considerations: bee swarm plot reveals distribution individual sales, horizontal lines show median prices property type across suburbs. analysis provides foundation understanding Leichhardt property market 2021. Potential extensions include: Analysing price trends multiple years Examining relationship price property features (bedrooms, bathrooms, land size) Investigating seasonal patterns sales Comparing Leichhardt prices Sydney areas

title: Analysing property prices by number of bedrooms in North Canberra suburbs
text: vignette demonstrates use allhomes package extract analyse historical property sales data allhomes.com.au. ’ll focus exploring relationship property prices number bedrooms three northern Canberra suburbs: Watson, Ainslie, Downer. analysing sales data 2018 2022, ’ll create visualisations understand price distributions across different bedroom counts. allhomes package provides access detailed sales data, including property features, sale prices, dates. example shows collect data multiple suburbs years, clean , perform basic exploratory data analysis.

title: Analysing property prices by number of bedrooms in North Canberra suburbs
text: First, load required packages. ’ll use allhomes Allhomes sales data extraction tidyverse data manipulation visualisation.
code: library(allhomes) library(tidyverse)

title: Analysing property prices by number of bedrooms in North Canberra suburbs
text: get_past_sales_data() function retrieves sales data specific suburb year. ’ll collect data three suburbs five years (2018-2022) using nested purrr::map_dfr approach.
code: # Define suburbs and years suburbs <- c("Watson, ACT", "Ainslie, ACT", "Downer, ACT") years <- 2018L:2022L  # Collect data using nested map_dfr data <- suburbs |>      map_dfr(function(burb)          map_dfr(years, function(yr)              get_past_sales_data(burb, yr) |>                  mutate(suburb_name = burb, sale_year = yr)))  # Check the structure of the collected data glimpse(data) #> Rows: 1,866 #> Columns: 24 #> $ contract_date          <date> 2018-12-17, 2018-10-18, 2018-11-12, 2018-11-02… #> $ address                <chr> "39/215 Aspinall Street", "13/21 Aspinall Stree… #> $ division               <chr> "Watson", "Watson", "Watson", "Watson", "Watson… #> $ state                  <chr> "ACT", "ACT", "ACT", "ACT", "ACT", "ACT", "ACT"… #> $ postcode               <chr> "2602", "2602", "2602", "2602", "2602", "2602",… #> $ url                    <chr> "https://www.allhomes.com.au/unit-39-215-aspina… #> $ property_type          <chr> "TOWNHOUSE", NA, NA, NA, "APARTMENT", NA, "UNIT… #> $ purpose                <chr> NA, "MULTIPLE UNIT DWELLING", "SINGLE RESIDENTI… #> $ bedrooms               <int> 3, NA, NA, NA, 2, NA, 1, 2, 3, 3, 4, 3, 4, 4, 3… #> $ bathrooms              <int> 2, NA, NA, NA, 2, NA, 1, 2, 2, 2, 3, 1, 2, 2, 2… #> $ parking                <int> 2, NA, NA, NA, 2, NA, 0, 2, 2, 2, 2, 2, 2, 2, 2… #> $ building_size          <int> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, 0, NA, NA, 0, NA, 2… #> $ block_size             <int> NA, 17228, 680, 2302, 20536, 830, 4365, 20536, … #> $ eer                    <dbl> 3.0, NA, NA, NA, 5.0, NA, 5.0, 4.0, 3.0, 3.0, 6… #> $ list_date              <date> 2018-11-28, NA, NA, NA, 2018-08-22, NA, 2018-1… #> $ transfer_date          <date> NA, 2018-12-17, 2018-12-17, 2018-12-17, 2018-1… #> $ days_on_market         <int> 19, NA, NA, NA, 82, NA, 29, 43, 34, 155, 23, 23… #> $ label                  <chr> "Sold", "Sold", "Sold", "Sold", "Sold", "Sold",… #> $ price                  <int> 595000, 516000, 715000, 368000, 360000, 760000,… #> $ agent                  <chr> "Independent Gungahlin", NA, NA, NA, "Sadil Qui… #> $ unimproved_value       <int> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, 567000, 557… #> $ unimproved_value_ratio <dbl> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, 0.7105263, … #> $ suburb_name            <chr> "Watson, ACT", "Watson, ACT", "Watson, ACT", "W… #> $ sale_year              <int> 2018, 2018, 2018, 2018, 2018, 2018, 2018, 2018,…

title: Analysing property prices by number of bedrooms in North Canberra suburbs
text: Let’s examine collected data. ’ll look total number sales, data completeness, basic statistics. Summary sales data suburb year
code: # Summary statistics data %>%     group_by(suburb_name, sale_year) |>     summarise(         total_sales = n(),         sales_with_bedrooms = sum(!is.na(bedrooms) & bedrooms > 0),         sales_with_price = sum(!is.na(price) & price > 0),         median_price = median(price, na.rm = TRUE),         .groups = "drop") |>     knitr::kable(caption = "Summary of sales data by suburb and year")

title: Analysing property prices by number of bedrooms in North Canberra suburbs
text: Now ’ll analyse property prices vary number bedrooms. ’ll filter valid data create boxplot visualise price distributions.
code: # Filter and prepare data for plotting plot_data <- data |>     filter(         !is.na(bedrooms), bedrooms > 0, bedrooms <= 6, # Reasonable bedroom range          !is.na(price), price > 0, price < 10e6)       # Reasonable price range  # Create boxplot plot_data |>     ggplot(aes(x = as.factor(bedrooms), y = price)) +     geom_boxplot() +     scale_y_continuous(         labels = scales::label_dollar(scale = 1e-6, suffix = "M")) +     facet_wrap(~ suburb_name) +     labs(         title = "Property sale prices by number of bedrooms",         subtitle = "Data from Watson, Ainslie, and Downer (ACT); 2018-2022",         x = "Number of bedrooms",         y = "Sale price (AUD)") +     theme_minimal()

title: Analysing property prices by number of bedrooms in North Canberra suburbs
text: visualisation, can observe: Price trends: Generally & unsurprisingly, properties bedrooms result higher sale prices, though exists significant variation within category. Suburb differences: Price distributions vary suburbs, reflecting local market conditions. Data considerations: sales records include bedroom information, outliers may affect distributions. analysis provides starting point deeper exploration. Potential extensions include: Analysing price per bedroom Examining trends time Including additional property features like bathrooms land size

title: Variability of the unimproved value per sqm across the ACT
text: vignette demonstrates combine geospatial property sales data analyse variability unimproved land values across Australian Capital Territory (ACT). ’ll use allhomes package extract historical property sales data, combine Australian Bureau Statistics (ABS) geospatial boundaries, create interactive visualisations understand spatial patterns land values. analysis focuses unimproved values per square metre across ACT suburbs, providing insights local property markets. integrating sales data statistical area boundaries, can create detailed spatial analyses reveal land values vary across different regions Canberra surrounding areas. Key packages used include: allhomes extracting Allhomes past sales data strayr ABS statistical area geometries leaflet interactive maps sf spatial data operations

title: Variability of the unimproved value per sqm across the ACT
text: Load required packages data extraction, spatial analysis, visualisation.
code: library(allhomes) library(tidyverse) library(strayr)    # Install with `remotes::install_github("runapp-aus/strayr")` library(sf) library(leaflet) library(htmltools)

title: Variability of the unimproved value per sqm across the ACT
text: obtain 2021 Statistical Area Level 2 (SA2) boundaries ABS strayr package. SA2 regions typically represent suburbs within cities. filter ACT regions clean area names removing territory suffixes. Using suburb names, collect past sales data 2021 2022 Allhomes. get_past_sales_data() function requires suburb names format “suburb_name, state/territory_abbreviation”.
code: # Get ACT SA2 boundaries data_spatial <- read_absmap("sa22021") |>     filter(state_name_2021 == "Australian Capital Territory") |>     mutate(sa2_name_2021 = str_remove_all(sa2_name_2021, "\\s\\(ACT\\).*$"))  # Extract unique suburb names for data collection sa2_names <- data_spatial |>      pull(sa2_name_2021) %>%      unique() |>      sort() |>     str_subset("-|No usual", negate = TRUE)  # Remove non-standard areas  # Display the suburbs we'll analyse sa2_names #>   [1] "Acton"               "Ainslie"             "Amaroo"              #>   [4] "Aranda"              "Arboretum"           "Banks"               #>   [7] "Barton"              "Belconnen"           "Black Mountain"      #>  [10] "Bonner"              "Bonython"            "Braddon"             #>  [13] "Bruce" #>  [70] "Kenny"               "Kingston"            "Kowen"               #>  [73] "Lake Burley Griffin" "Latham"              "Lawson"              #>  [76] "Lyneham"             "Lyons" "Pearce"              #> [103] "Phillip"             "Red Hill"            "Reid"                #> [106] "Richardson"          "Rivett"              "Russell"             #> [109] "Scrivener" title: Variability of the unimproved value per sqm across the ACT
text: obtain 2021 Statistical Area Level 2 (SA2) boundaries ABS strayr package. SA2 regions typically represent suburbs within cities. filter ACT regions clean area names removing territory suffixes.
code: # Get ACT SA2 boundaries data_spatial <- read_absmap("sa22021") |>     filter(state_name_2021 == "Australian Capital Territory") |>     mutate(sa2_name_2021 = str_remove_all(sa2_name_2021, "\\s\\(ACT\\).*$"))  # Extract unique suburb names for data collection sa2_names <- data_spatial |>      pull(sa2_name_2021) %>%      unique() |>      sort() |>     str_subset("-|No usual", negate = TRUE)  # Remove non-standard areas  # Display the suburbs we'll analyse sa2_names #>   [1] "Acton"               "Ainslie"             "Amaroo"              #>   [4] "Aranda"              "Arboretum" title: Variability of the unimproved value per sqm across the ACT
text: Using suburb names, collect past sales data 2021 2022 Allhomes. get_past_sales_data() function requires suburb names format “suburb_name, state/territory_abbreviation”.
code: # Collect sales data for all ACT suburbs (2021-2022) # This may take several minutes depending on the number of suburbs data_allhomes <- sa2_names |>      paste0(", ACT") |>      map_dfr(function(suburb)          map_dfr(2021L:2022L, function(year)              get_past_sales_data(suburb, year))) #> Warning in get_past_sales_data(suburb, year): No sales data for suburb 'Acton, title: Variability of the unimproved value per sqm across the ACT
text: Let’s examine collected data understand structure completeness. Summary sales data ACT suburb (2021-2022)
code: # Summary statistics by suburb data_summary <- data_allhomes %>%     group_by(division) |>     summarise(         total_sales = n(),         sales_with_uv = sum(!is.na(unimproved_value) & unimproved_value > 0),         sales_with_block_size = sum(!is.na(block_size) & block_size > 0),         median_uv = median(unimproved_value, na.rm = TRUE),         median_block_size = median(block_size, na.rm = TRUE),         .groups = "drop") |>     arrange(division)  # Display summary table data_summary |>     knitr::kable(         caption = "Summary of sales data by ACT suburb (2021-2022)",         digits = 0,         format.args = list(big.mark = ","))

title: Variability of the unimproved value per sqm across the ACT
text: combine geospatial boundaries sales data, filtering valid property sales calculating unimproved value per square metre. focus residential properties block sizes 100-2000 sqm positive unimproved values exclude commercial properties outliers. Create interactive Leaflet map showing median unimproved values per square metre across ACT suburbs. map includes hover information showing 95% confidence interval area.
code:
# Join spatial and sales data, calculate UV per sqm statistics data_combined <- data_spatial |> inner_join( data_allhomes |> # Filter for residential properties filter(between(block_size, 100, 2000), unimproved_value > 100) |> # Calculate UV per square metre mutate(uv_per_sqm = unimproved_value / block_size) |> # Calculate quantiles by suburb reframe( uv_per_sqm = quantile(uv_per_sqm, probs = c(0.025, 0.5, 0.975), na.rm = TRUE), quantile = c("lower_95", "median", "upper_95"), .by = division) |> # Reshape to wide format for mapping pivot_wider(names_from = "quantile", values_from = "uv_per_sqm"), by = c("sa2_name_2021" = "division")) # Create color palette based on median UV values pal <- colorNumeric("YlOrRd", domain = data_combined$median) # Generate interactive map leaflet(data = data_combined, height = 600) %>% addTiles() %>% addPolygons( fillColor = ~pal(median), fillOpacity = 0.7, color = "white", weight = 1, smoothFactor = 0.2, highlightOptions = highlightOptions( weight = 5, color = "#666", fillOpacity = 0.7, bringToFront = TRUE), label = sprintf( "%s
Median UV/m²: %s
95%% CI: [%s, %s]", data_combined$sa2_name_2021, sprintf("$%.0f", data_combined$median), sprintf("$%.0f", data_combined$lower_95), sprintf("$%.0f", data_combined$upper_95)) %>% map(HTML), labelOptions = labelOptions( style = list("font-weight" = "normal", padding = "3px 8px"), textsize = "15px", direction = "auto")) %>% addLegend( pal = pal, values = ~median, opacity = 0.7, title = "Unimproved Value (UV) per m²", position = "bottomright")

title: Variability of the unimproved value per sqm across the ACT
text: combine geospatial boundaries sales data, filtering valid property sales calculating unimproved value per square metre. focus residential properties block sizes 100-2000 sqm positive unimproved values exclude commercial properties outliers.
code: # Join spatial and sales data, calculate UV per sqm statistics data_combined <- data_spatial |>     inner_join(         data_allhomes |>             # Filter for residential properties             filter(between(block_size, 100, 2000), unimproved_value > 100) |>             # Calculate UV per square metre             mutate(uv_per_sqm = unimproved_value / block_size) |>             # Calculate quantiles by suburb             reframe(                 uv_per_sqm = quantile(uv_per_sqm, probs = c(0.025, 0.5, 0.975), na.rm = TRUE),                 quantile = c("lower_95", "median", "upper_95"),                 .by = division) |>             # Reshape to wide format for mapping             pivot_wider(names_from = "quantile", values_from = "uv_per_sqm"),         by = c("sa2_name_2021" = "division"))

title: Variability of the unimproved value per sqm across the ACT
text: Create interactive Leaflet map showing median unimproved values per square metre across ACT suburbs. map includes hover information showing 95% confidence interval area.
code:
# Create color palette based on median UV values pal <- colorNumeric("YlOrRd", domain = data_combined$median) # Generate interactive map leaflet(data = data_combined, height = 600) %>% addTiles() %>% addPolygons( fillColor = ~pal(median), fillOpacity = 0.7, color = "white", weight = 1, smoothFactor = 0.2, highlightOptions = highlightOptions( weight = 5, color = "#666", fillOpacity = 0.7, bringToFront = TRUE), label = sprintf( "%s
Median UV/m²: %s
95%% CI: [%s, %s]", data_combined$sa2_name_2021, sprintf("$%.0f", data_combined$median), sprintf("$%.0f", data_combined$lower_95), sprintf("$%.0f", data_combined$upper_95)) %>% map(HTML), labelOptions = labelOptions( style = list("font-weight" = "normal", padding = "3px 8px"), textsize = "15px", direction = "auto")) %>% addLegend( pal = pal, values = ~median, opacity = 0.7, title = "Unimproved Value (UV) per m²", position = "bottomright")

title: Variability of the unimproved value per sqm across the ACT
text: spatial analysis unimproved land values across ACT, can observe: Geographic variation: Clear spatial patterns land values across Canberra suburbs, reflecting proximity major urban centers (Civic, Woden, Belconnen, etc.), amenities, local market conditions Value ranges: 95% confidence intervals show variability within suburb, highlighting areas consistent vs. diverse land values Data quality: analysis excludes commercial properties outliers focus residential land values approach provides foundation understanding ACT property markets spatially. Potential extensions include: Analysing trends multiple years identify value changes Incorporating additional property features (bedrooms, land size categories) Comparing capital cities using similar methodologies Investigating correlations demographic economic indicators

what: Extract Past Sales Data from Allhomes.com.au
title: Extract Past Sales Data from Allhomes.com.au
text: goal allhomes extract past sales data specific suburb(s) year(s) Australian property website allhomes.com.au. Allhomes data include address property details, date price sale, block size unimproved value properties mainly ACT NSW.

title: Extract Past Sales Data from Allhomes.com.au
text: can install allhomes GitHub : (Note: package longer available CRAN.)
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("mevers/allhomes")

title: Extract Past Sales Data from Allhomes.com.au
text: main function get_past_sales_data(), extracts past sales data given suburb year. Note: March 2026, function arguments suburb year must scalars. Users responsible looping multiple suburbs /years needed.
code: library(allhomes)  # Get sales data for Balmain, NSW in 2019 sales <- get_past_sales_data("Balmain, NSW", 2019) sales ## A tibble: 286 × 22 #   contract_date address     division state postcode url   property_type purpose bedrooms bathrooms parking building_size block_size #   <date>        <chr>       <chr>    <chr> <chr>    <chr> <chr>         <chr>      <int>     <int>   <int> <lgl>              <int> # 1 2019-11-16    2 Adolphus… Balmain  NSW   2041     http… NA            "RESID…       NA        NA      NA NA                   234 # 2 NA            1 Wisbeach… Balmain  NSW   2041     http… HOUSE          NA            1         1       0 NA                    NA # 3 2019-11-19    75/24 Buch… Balmain  NSW   2041     http… NA            "RESID…       NA        NA      NA NA                    NA # 4 2019-12-23    73-79 Beat… Balmain  NSW   2041     http… NA            "RESID…       NA        NA      NA NA                   278 # 5 2019-10-24    4 Gladston… Balmain  NSW   2041     http… HOUSE         "RESID…        2         1       0 NA                    87 # 6 2019-11-07    128/85 Rey… Balmain  NSW   2041     http… NA            "RESID…       NA        NA      NA NA                    NA # 7 2019-11-01    21 Phillip… Balmain  NSW   2041     http… HOUSE         "RESID…        2         2       0 NA                   127 # 8 2019-11-24    43 Beattie… Balmain  NSW   2041     http… NA            "RESID…       NA        NA      NA NA                   133 # 9 NA            491 Darlin… Balmain  NSW   2041     http… NA            ""            NA        NA      NA NA                   118 #10 2019-11-15    26 Little … Balmain  NSW   2041     http… NA            "RESID…       NA        NA      NA NA                   126 ## ℹ 276 more rows ## ℹ 9 more variables: eer <int>, list_date <date>, transfer_date <date>, days_on_market <int>, label <chr>, price <int>, ##   agent <chr>, unimproved_value <int>, unimproved_value_ratio <dbl> ## ℹ Use `print(n = ...)` to see more rows

title: Extract Past Sales Data from Allhomes.com.au
text: Changes Allhomes website required data extraction method shift scraping (static) HTML pages using Allhomes’ GraphQL API. change leverages website’s dynamic data loading via GraphQL endpoint (/graphql), provides structured JSON responses sales history data. Key improvements include: Direct API Access: Instead parsing rendered HTML, package now queries GraphQL API persisted queries variables (e.g., locality slug, filters, pagination). Reliability: GraphQL allows consistent data retrieval, reducing risk breakage website layout changes. However, reliability depends SHA256 hash persisted queries changing; server-side hash changes, requests fail. Efficiency: Paginated data fetched directly, avoiding need scrape multiple HTML pages. technical details GraphQL implementation, refer data extraction brief.

title: Extract Past Sales Data from Allhomes.com.au
text: allhomes also provides two datasets: divisions_ACT: Division names IDs ACT. divisions_NSW: Division names IDs NSW.

title: Extract historical Allhomes past sales data for an ACT/NSW suburb. — get_past_sales_data
text: Extract historical Allhomes past sales data ACT/NSW suburb.

title: Extract historical Allhomes past sales data for an ACT/NSW suburb. — get_past_sales_data
code: get_past_sales_data(suburb, year, max_entries = 5000)

title: Extract historical Allhomes past sales data for an ACT/NSW suburb. — get_past_sales_data
text: suburb character scalar denoting specific suburb. Format must "<suburb_name>, <state/territory_abbreviation>", e.g. "Balmain, ACT". year integer scalar denoting year. NULL, historical data suburb returned. Otherwise sales data indicated year returned. max_entries maximum number records returned. Defaults 5000.
